title: Now Im confused
wikitext:
[]: Now I'm confused             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Sat Jul 15, 2006 8:20 am I just watched the episode which talked about the last Angel. It's said that Seele sent Kaworu to Nerv in order to trigger the Third Impact. But I think both Seele and Nerv clearly know that the creature in the Central Dogma wasn't Adam, it was Lilith, right? And wasn't it said that to trigger the Third Impact Adam must be involved? Then if Seele knew that sending Kaworu to contact Lilith wouldn't trigger anything, what's the point in sending him to death? Was Seele too eager to destroy all Angels as soon as possible so that they could carry out their own conspiracy? Or am I having some misconception? Please correct me.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 15, 2006 8:51 am Everything you've said is right. It all comes down to a pretty obvious plot hole- In the Director's Cut edition, SEELE tell Kaworu that the creature in Terminal Dogma is in fact Lilith, and that Ikari has joined with Adam. But when Kaworu reaches TD, he's STILL shocked to see it's Lilith. I'm not sure why they added the Kaworu-SEELE scene, because it certainly only complicated things FURTHER. However, if you ignore that scene, one could claim SEELE misinformed Kaworu, or simply didn't know Kaworu was planning on going down to Terminal Dogma.

[]:             Quote Post by Alfonso » Sat Jul 15, 2006 9:17 am Or that they were simply sending him, the Last Angel, to his death so that there were no possible threats to a human instigated Third Impact. They knew he was going to die, most likely. They were probably just following the events as detailed in the Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 15, 2006 9:31 am Legend of the Past wrote: Everything you've said is right. It all comes down to a pretty obvious plot hole- In the Director's Cut edition, SEELE tell Kaworu that the creature in Terminal Dogma is in fact Lilith, and that Ikari has joined with Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Alfonso » Sat Jul 15, 2006 9:52 am In reply to Reichu, since I suck and forgot to just quote. D: Is it possible that Kaworu simply believed what he was told? Misato and everyone believed that the white giant on the red cross was Adam because that's what they were told. As for why Kaworu couldn't sense it wasn't Adam, perhaps he sensed a 'Seed of Life' and since he was previously told that Adam was down there, just assumed that that was Her. Not sure if you were actually asking, or simply proposing the question, but I thought I'd share my thoughts on the matter anyway. We never get to see if Tabris has any funky moves of his own. Considering his limited form as a human, one can assume that he doesn't have the same combat skills as his brothers and sisters: And so uses his unique ability to control an Eva as a form of protection against whoever might try to stop him making contact with his Mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jul 15, 2006 11:25 am But there is still this: ep24' platinum sub wrote: IKARI: Stop it from invading Terminal Dogma at all costs. Misato (OFF): But why did the Angel take Unit 02? FUYUTSUKI: Could it be planning to merge with Unit 02?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 15, 2006 12:18 pm I tend to think seele sent angelboy their for two reasons. 1) to mess wth Shinji mind so he'd be less of a thrreat if he was in the plug when they tryed to used unit one as conduit to lilith 2) to die so the way was clear for third impact

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 15, 2006 2:38 pm @Ornette: There's also... [code:1]Shinji: Kaworu, stop this! Why?! KAWORU: The Eva are made of the same body as I am. Because I am born from Adam as well. I could merge with them, if only they didn't have souls. Unit 02's soul has shut itself away for now.[/code:1] ...and the reiteration in NGE2 CI. Ornette wrote: Doesn't it seem like he was going to do something funky with Eva-02?
